steps.title: Use the intercept clue first
steps.content: A $y$-intercept always has $x=0$. The intercept is written as $(k-5,b)$, so $k-5=0$ and $k=5$. After substituting $k=5$ into the table, the two points on the line are $(5,13)$ and $(12,-15)$.

steps.title: Fit the line in Desmos
steps.content: Enter the two points as lists and run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=-4$ and $b=33$, so the line is $y=-4x+33$.

steps.title: Answer b
steps.content: The number $b$ is the $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept. Since the fitted line has intercept $(0,33)$, the value of $b$ is 33.
